name: Chesapeake Oyster Bisque
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice onions.
2. Dice celery.
3. Drain oysters and save the liquid. Oysters may be chopped or left whole as desired. Add water to the oyster liquid to make 1 quart.
4. In a stock pot, combine bay leaf, one onion, and simmer for about 1 hour uncovered.
5. Remove from heat; allow to cool for about 1 hour, strain, and set aside.
6. In butter, saute remaining onion and celery; cook for about 5 minutes or until pale in color.
7. Add flour and stir well, but do not brown.
8. Add strained oyster stock: stir well to prevent lumps from forming.
9. Add the remaining stock and heat until hot and thickened (about 10-15 minutes).
10. Add oysters and cream. Heat about 5 more minutes.
11. Add sherry and ladle into serving bowls. Garnish with chopped parsley.
